title: fix: update Buffer Instagram media payload
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Updates Buffer image and carousel payloads to the current ordered `assets` input.
- Uses Instagram post metadata for multi-image carousels; Buffer now rejects `type: carousel`.
- Preserves the existing single-image and multi-image publishing behavior.

Root cause: Buffer changed both its media input shape and accepted Instagram post types. Renewing the API key restores authentication, but the old mutation payload is no longer valid.

Checks:
- Buffer API authentication and all four configured channels verified read-only
- Single-image and carousel payload assertions passed
- Python compilation passed
- Live Instagram draft mutation tested and cleaned up
- `git diff --check` passed

gist.headline: Fixing the social pipes
gist.blurb: Updated our internal Buffer scripts to handle their new Instagram media payload format. The daily social posts will keep flowing smoothly.
gist.summary: Updated the internal Buffer publishing scripts to use the new ordered assets input and Instagram post metadata for multi-image carousels.
gist.impact: Internal social media pipelines can successfully publish single-image and carousel posts to Instagram again.
gist.visual_concept: A cute pixel art bee mascot wearing a tiny hard hat, fixing a glowing pipe or conveyor belt that has little polaroid pictures (Instagram) flowing through it. A chunky robot with a monitor head is helping by holding a wrench.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny hard hat, fixing a glowing lime green (#ecf874) pipeline that transports small polaroid pictures. A chunky robot with a CRT monitor head helps by holding a wrench. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
